artPrompt: A reckoning at the carnival counter: a heavy ledger lying open with blank smudged pages glistening dark red, a clawed accountant's hand resting upon it, hot-gold lamplight and ember sparks bleeding through carnival-red haze, tattered tents and rigged wheels fading into smoky black, the dreadful settling of accounts rendered with tender grim stillness, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Settle the Tab in Blood
text: The carnival's accounts come due all at once. Each player's life total is divided in half (round however the player to your left feels like). Then each player who has ever, in this game, refused to share a snack with you loses an ADDITIONAL 3 life. You decide who that is. There is no appeal. The carnival keeps no records but never forgets.
flavorText: The ledger is written in a substance that is technically ink and emotionally something worse.
